reels.caption: 🇺🇸 Knockouts create instant emotion. They require no context and no explanation. In an era dominated by TikTok, Instagra
good: Hook is visually arresting: bold red all-caps text with dynamic strike action immediately dominates viewport
High share-to-view ratio (45 shares / 1109 views = 4%) shows controversial topic drives distribution
Bilingual captions (English + Portuguese) tap into core UFC demographic and expand addressable audience
Strong emotional trigger: violence and knockouts are inherently high-engagement content
bad: Extremely low saves (1 save = 0.09% of views) indicates no lasting reference value or actionable takeaway
Reach only 67% of views (741/1109) suggests algorithm isn't pushing beyond initial audience
Meta-commentary ending about dopamine/scrolling feels tangential and dilutes the core sports message
Dense overlaid text may overwhelm viewers during quick scrolls despite bold formatting
reels.top_tip: Replace the dopamine/phone ending with a clear value prop—a poll ('Are strikers the future of MMA?'), a question, or technique insight—to drive saves and signal to the algorithm that content has lasting value, not just shareability

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Cinco anos parado fazem diferença? Para o Tailandês, a resposta real vem no segundo round, quando a explosão inicial
good: Strong immediate hook: close-up facial reaction fills frame in first 0.5s — proven scroll-stopper for reaction content
Curiosity-driven bilingual caption frames a specific question ('Does 5 years away matter?') that compels watch-through
Excellent watch retention: 7.9s average on short-form content shows narrative holds attention despite low overall reach
Clear UFC branding establishes immediate context and appeals to fight sports audience from frame 1
bad: Zero shares + 1 save despite 24.4% engagement = viewers stay but don't spread; narrative lacks a debatable take or controversial opinion
Very low algorithmic reach (262 from 406 views) indicates poor seeding; hook text overlay missing and hashtag strategy likely weak
Abrupt transition between reaction commentary (frames 1-2) and championship belts (frame 3) breaks narrative momentum and confuses message
No on-screen graphics or predictions during mid-video; relies entirely on caption to explain stakes, losing viewers who don't read
reels.top_tip: Add an on-screen prediction or opinion overlay mid-video ('Round 2 cardio matters' or 'Age shows here') to give viewers a debate-worthy take they'll share with fight fan communities

reels.caption:
PG’s Top 5 Knockouts — Part 1 💥
Some knockouts don’t even look real 😳 The first edition is here and we’re just getting s
good: Strong hook with UFC octagon action immediately visible—scroll-stopping visual impact
Fighter names on-screen build credibility and help viewers identify the content
Series framing ('Part 1') + follow CTA drives audience retention and follow-backs
5.8s average watch time is solid—viewers stay through most of the clip
bad: Zero saves and only 1 share despite 12% engagement—content entertains but isn't compelling enough to bookmark/amplify
Actual knockout moments lack cinematic treatment (no slow-motion replay, zoom, or dramatic angle emphasis)
Mid-video transitions to darker scene with crowd reaction instead of emphasizing the finishing blow in sharp detail
Relies on text overlays for impact rather than dynamic editing that highlights the KO itself
reels.top_tip: Slow-motion replay of each knockout with zoom or dramatic angle—the actual finishing move is the most share-worthy moment but gets glossed over; make the knockout the visual centerpiece with 0.5-1s of cinematic slow-mo per finish

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Gable Steveson é campeão olímpico de wrestling, treina com Jon Jones e está 3-0 no MMA. Mas será que o UFC está realm
good: Strong hook: 'MERITO OU MARKETING?' question creates immediate curiosity, drives 4.5s average watch-through
Visual impact in frame 1: Close-up face with dramatic artistic background fills frame and stops scrolls
Bilingual caption (Portuguese + English) expands potential audience reach
bad: Zero saves and only 1 share — viewers watch but don't bookmark or spread; debate framing invites argument rather than shareability
CTA buried in caption only — 'O que você acha?' doesn't appear as on-screen text, so casual viewers miss it
Mid-video momentum drops: subject's expression becomes neutral/tired, losing the hook's intensity
reels.top_tip: Add on-screen text or Instagram poll sticker mid-video asking 'Merit or Marketing?' or 'Share your take' — convert passive watchers into sharers by making the opinion-share frictionless and visible
